kernel_5op.intake: kick_bench fix landed 2-RC ladder (outer __RC + inner __HEXA_RC) → audit propagation across remaining `; echo __RC=$?` exec sites in nexus/tool. Bug class: shell-wrapped exec captures only outer rc, misses inner runtime failures (rc=3 from hexa_interp inside docker), causing per-record `status:fire` to be misleading.

if a site gates a decision, the 3-way (or collapsed 2-way) outer/inner ladder is applied
if a site is log-only, honestly no-action with documented rationale
nexus/tool/*.hexa sweep finds ALL `; echo __RC=$?` instances (no false-negative)
each touched tool passes its --selftest or --dry-run
no paint-over of already-correct sites (kick_bench.hexa L471 already fixed → not re-edited)

pattern_used: Outer/inner separation. Outer rc parsed via first-line-after-__RC= (defense against trailing __HEXA_RC= bleed). Inner rc parsed via last_index_of(__HEXA_RC=) + boundary-scan ([space, newline, tab, underscore]) + safe int conversion. For binary gate sites (absorb/spawn/sweep), collapsed 2-way: FAIL on either; preserves separate reason slug (ingest-rc-N vs ingest-inner-rc-N) for raw-66 observability. For status-field sites (kick_bench), full 3-way ladder {fire, fail, fail-inner-rc}.

regression_results.stdout: [witness_emit selftest] OK — classifier 2 cases / happy-path emit / idempotent re-emit

tier1_promotions.rationale: All 6 gating exec sites in nexus/tool that wrap hexa-bin in shell `; echo __RC=$?` now parse BOTH outer __RC= AND inner __HEXA_RC= (auto-appended by hexa.real exec()). Outer-rc-only gates were a bug-class allowing inner runtime failures (e.g. dispatched docker rc=3) to silently PASS upstream gates. Pattern carried forward from kick_bench.hexa post-fix as canonical reference.

Outer rc_str must take first-line-only after __RC= to avoid bleeding into the trailing __HEXA_RC= sentinel that hexa.real appends. Without this guard, rc_str literal becomes "0\n__HEXA_RC=N" and string-equality comparison `rc_str != "0"` always fails → false FAIL gating. atlas_absorb_hook/witness_emit/atlas_absorb_sweeper all needed this guard since they used string-comparison (vs kick_bench which uses _safe_to_int_line that already takes first line).

For binary absorb-or-skip gates, full 3-way ladder (fire/fail/fail-inner-rc) is overkill. Collapsed to 2-way FAIL with separate reason slug per branch (ingest-rc-N vs ingest-inner-rc-N) preserves observability without status-field bloat. Reserved for sites where the gate is exit-or-continue, not status-classification.

carry_forward: b-dispatch-rc-shared-helper: extract _extract_inner_hexa_rc to a shared lib (lib/hexa_rc.hexa or similar) so atlas_absorb_hook/witness_emit/atlas_absorb_sweeper don't each open-code the same boundary-scan loop. Currently 3× ~22-line duplication. Defer until 2nd duplicated bug emerges (raw 14 wait-for-pattern).

b-cross-repo-sweep: nexus/tool/ scope only this cycle. Other repos (hexa-lang/hive/seer) likely have same `; echo __RC=$?` pattern but cross-repo edits were NOT in scope per kick directive (only fix if bug class found there). Recommend separate ω-cycle to grep + audit cross-repo.
b-runtime-emission: hexa.real auto-appends __HEXA_RC= but the format is implicit. Should be documented in a .n6 atlas append or hexa-runtime spec so future shell-wrapped exec sites know to parse it.
